title: Housekeeping Assistance * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Housekeeping Assistance * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions 23 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Older Adults Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON HERMISTON OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care to older adults living in their homes. Services include housekeeping, personal care, laundry, shopping, and meal preparation. Also offers case management to help older adults make cost-effective and appropriate decisions regarding their care needs. Service hours per person vary depending on assessed need and local funding amounts. Hours may range from 5-20 hours per week. Some examples of when Oregon Project Independence may be appropriate: -When an individual requires personal and home care assistance due to injury or illness -When an individual has been hospitalized and needs help while recovering in their home 1565 N 1st Street Suite 1 Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: -Age 60 and older, and individuals of any age diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease or a related disorder -Ages 19-59 individuals who have physical disabilities Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-noon Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON HERMISTON OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care. Services include housekeeping, personal care, laundry, shopping, transportation, and meal preparation and delivery. Adult day services (non-residential community-based programs, providing services for part of the day). Registered nurse services (one-time or intermittent assessment, service planning, medication monitoring, provider training, and education). Also offers case management and options counseling (counseling to support informed decision-making on services based on strengths, needs, and preferences). Service hours per person vary depending on assessed need and funding amounts. Hours may range from 5-20 hours a month. Benefits will not be subject to estate recovery. 1565 N 1st Street Suite 1 Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: Adults, age 18 and older, who meet the following criteria: -Need help from another person with personal care tasks -Income no greater than 400% of the Federal Poverty Level -Assets no greater than the average cost of six months in a nursing facility -Currently living in their own home and not a provider-operator or owned facility or foster/care home Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-noon (541) 289-7755 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON MAIN OFFICE OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care to older adults living in their homes. Also offers case management and options counseling (counseling to support informed decision-making on services based on strengths, needs, and preferences). Service hours per person vary depending on assessed need and funding amounts. Participants may receive up to 20 hours of caregiving assistance per week. Benefits will not be subject to estate recovery. 5240 NE Elam Young Parkway Suite 300 Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Adults, age 18 and older, who meet the following criteria: -Need help from another person with personal care tasks -Income no greater than 400% of the federal poverty Level -Assets no greater than the average cost of six months in a nursing facility -Currently living in their own home and not a provider-operator or owned facility or foster/care home Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 846-3060 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF
